description: Canonical terminology. All app content must reference only these forms.

terms.meaning: Light of God (Jyoti + Isha). Vedic science of the celestial lights; Vedic astrology.
Sanskrit name for the Vedanga dealing with astronomy/astrology.
Limbs of the Vedas — six auxiliary disciplines: Siksha, Kalpa, Vyakarana, Nirukta, Chhanda, Jyotisha.
Three branches of Jyotish: Siddhanta (astronomy/math), Samhita (mundane), Hora (predictive/natal).
Six limbs of Jyotish: Jataka, Prasna, Muhurta, Nimitta, Ganita, Gola.
Mathematical astrology — calculation.
Predictive astrology — interpretation.
Natal astrology (birth chart).
Horary / question-based astrology.
Electional astrology (choosing auspicious timing).
Mundane astrology (nations, weather, disasters).
Zodiac sign. Literally 'heap' or 'cluster of stars'. 360° divided into 12 equal 30° segments.
The wheel of 12 signs.
House; literally 'state of being'. 12 equal divisions calculated from the Ascendant.
Planet. Literally 'to grasp / seize'. 9 grahas are used in Jyotish (Navagrahas).
The 9 planets: Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu.
Lunar mansion. 27 divisions of 13°20' each along the ecliptic, defined by the Moon's path.
Quarter of a nakshatra (3°20'). Each nakshatra has 4 padas; 27×4 = 108 padas. Each pada maps to one navamsa.
Ascendant. The exact degree of the zodiac rising on the eastern horizon at birth. Also called Udhaya Lagna / Janma Lagna.
Rising sign — synonym of Janma Lagna.
Moon's sign treated as the 1st house for analysis.
Sun's sign treated as the 1st house for analysis.
The Moon's nakshatra at birth. Used for naming, Vimshottari Dasha start, and Tarabalam.
The Moon's sign at birth (same as Chandra Rasi).
Birth chart / horoscope.
Significator — the planet or body that signifies a particular area of life. 'Karakatwa' = significations.
The significations or domains a planet governs.
Self-indicator. The planet with highest degree in the chart (Jaimini). Signifies the soul.
Moveable significators (Jaimini): 8 planets (excluding Ketu) ranked by longitude — Atmakaraka, Amatyakaraka, Bhratrikaraka, Matrikaraka, Pitrikaraka, Putrakaraka, Gnatikaraka, Darakaraka.
Exaltation — a planet's strongest sign placement.
Debilitation — a planet's weakest sign placement. 180° from exaltation.
A specific degree range within a planet's own sign where it gives maximum Trikona strength.
Own sign. Placement in a planet's own sign (distinct from the Moolatrikona degree range).
Directional strength. Planet is strong in a specific house: Mercury+Jupiter in 1st (East), Moon+Venus in 4th (North), Saturn in 7th (West), Sun+Mars in 10th (South).
Natural (permanent) planetary friendship. Based on positions from Moolatrikona sign.
Temporal (chart-specific) planetary friendship. Based on positions in the actual chart.
Five-fold compound relationship combining Naisargika and Tatkalika: Adhi-Mitra, Mitra, Sama, Satru, Adhi-Satru.
State of a planet. Two main systems: Baladi (5 degree-based infant→dead states) and Deeptadi (9 dignity-based states).
Aspect — planets 'looking at' houses/planets from their position. Universal 7th + special for Mars/Jupiter/Saturn.
Conjunction — two or more planets in the same sign/house.
Combustion. A planet too close to the Sun in the same sign becomes astanga and loses power.
A planet in the sign immediately preceding its exaltation sign — 'desiring exaltation'.
A planet in the sign immediately preceding its debilitation sign.
Planet ascending from debilitation toward exaltation — gaining strength.
Planet descending from exaltation toward debilitation — losing strength.
A planet or Lagna occupying the same sign in both Rasi (D1) and Navamsa (D9). Gives extra strength.
Specific auspicious degrees in each sign that give favourable results when a planet is posited there.
Jaimini: the sign in the Navamsa chart occupied by the Atmakaraka. The 12th from this shows Ishta Devata.
Angular / cardinal house. 1, 4, 7, 10. Houses of Vishnu (sustenance).
Trine / triangular house. 1, 5, 9. Houses of Lakshmi (fortune).
Succedent house. 2, 5, 8, 11. Medium strength.
Cadent / declining house. 3, 6, 9, 12. Weakest (except 9th which is a Trikona).
Growth house. 3, 6, 10, 11. Malefics perform well here; results improve with age.
Difficult house. 6, 8, 12.
Death-inflicting house. 2, 7. Used for longevity analysis.
A planet that lords both a Kendra AND a Trikona for a given Lagna. Gives Rajayoga.
Combinations indicating power, success, fortune. Typically formed by Kendra–Trikona lord association.
The blemish where a natural benefic loses beneficence by lording a Kendra. Strong for dual-sign Lagnas (Ge/Vi/Sa/Pi).
Exchange yoga — when lord of house A is in house B, and lord of B is in A. Strengthens both houses.
Obstructing house. 11th for movable Lagnas, 9th for fixed, 7th for dual.
Technique of examining a house from itself by counting the same relative position from it.
The planet ruling the sign in which another planet is placed.
Planetary period. Vimshottari Dasha is a 120-year cycle starting from the planet ruling Janma Nakshatra.
Major planetary period in Vimshottari system.
Sub-period within a Mahadasha.
Transit — current movement of planets relative to the birth chart.
Strength of the daily nakshatra relative to one's Janma Nakshatra. 9-fold grouping: Janma, Sampat, Vipat, Kshema, Pratyak, Sadhaka, Naidhana, Mitra, Adhi-Mitra.
Cycle. Refers to each of the 3 groups of 9 nakshatras.
Four aims of life: Dharma, Artha, Kama, Moksha.
Righteousness, duty, moral order.
Wealth, economic values, career.
Pleasures, desires, relationships.
Liberation, self-realisation.
Cosmic Man. The Zodiac personified as a body — Aries (head) to Pisces (feet).
Quality. Three gunas: Sattva (harmony), Rajas (activity), Tamas (inertia).
Element. Five: Prithvi (earth), Jala/Apas (water), Agni/Tejas (fire), Vayu (air), Akasha (ether).
Five great elements (same as tattvas).
In Ayurveda, body humour: Vata (air), Pitta (fire), Kapha (water). In astrology, also means blemish/flaw.
Class/order: Brahmin (scholars), Kshatriya (warriors), Vaishya (traders), Shudra (workers), Mlechha (outcaste).
Nakshatra temperament class: Deva (divine), Manushya (human), Rakshasa (demon). Used in marriage compatibility.
Animal symbol of a nakshatra; used in marriage compatibility.
Power/energy. Each nakshatra has its unique shakti.
Movable (used for cardinal signs and moveable nakshatras).
Fixed.
Dual / mutable / common nature.
Harsh / malefic.
Gentle / benefic.
Head-rising sign. Rises with its beginning portion first — auspicious, strong in daytime.
Back-rising sign. Rises with its ending portion first — inauspicious, strong at night.
Both-way-rising. Only Pisces. Changeable.
Biped (human) sign — favours the 1st house.
Quadruped sign — favours the 10th house.
Reptile/crawling sign — favours the 7th house.
Watery sign — favours the 4th house.
Angular difference between Tropical and Sidereal zodiacs. Currently ~24°. Jyotish uses Nirayana (sidereal).
Tropical zodiac — 'with movement'. Season-based, Vernal Equinox = 0° Aries.
Sidereal zodiac — 'without movement'. Fixed to stars, Ashwini = 0° Aries.
Zodiac belt — 'shining wheel'.
Sun's northward journey (Dec–June).
Sun's southward journey (June–Dec).
Hindu almanac — tracks lunar days (tithi), solar months, eclipses, planetary motions.
Bright (waxing) lunar fortnight.
Dark (waning) lunar fortnight.
Waning Moon. Moon is malefic from 8th day of Krishna Paksha to 8th day of Shukla Paksha.
Division (of a sign). Used in divisional chart naming.
Divisional chart. 16 vargas listed in BPHS (D1, D2, D3, ... D60).
9-division chart. Each sign divided into 9 amsas of 3°20'. Used for spouse, strength, dharma.
2-division chart. Sun's Hora vs Moon's Hora. Wealth/sustenance analysis.
10-division chart for career/profession.
Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra. Foundational text of Vedic astrology by Rishi Parashara.
Classical text by Rishi Jaimini. Introduces Chara Karakas, Rashi Drishti, and Chara Dasha.
Varshaphala (annual return) system influenced by Arabic/Western traditions.
Palm leaf manuscript predictive system.
Krishnamurti Paddhathi. 20th century system using sub-lord theory and Placidus houses.
